subject: Calculus Prerequisite
question: Investigate the convergence of $\int^2 \frac{1}{(x-2)^2}\,dx$.
explanation: We deal with this improper integral as follows $\int^2 \frac{1}{(x- 2)^2}\,dx = \lim_{t\to 2^-} \int^t \frac{1}{(x- 2)^2}\,dx = \lim_{t\to 2^-} -\frac{1}{(x- 2)}\Big|^t = \lim_{t\to 2^-} \left(-\frac{1}{t- 2} - \frac{1}{2}\right) = \infty$. So that the given improper integral is divergent
